summary: The project-setup-and-database feature is fully implemented and working correctly. The Next.js 15 project is properly configured with TypeScript strict mode, Tailwind CSS v4, and ESLint. Supabase SSR clients (server.ts, client.ts, middleware.ts) are correctly set up with session refresh and protected route redirects. All 9 database tables have been created with appropriate indexes and RLS policies. The seed data includes 8 CC-to-university transfer paths (exceeding the required 5-10), with SMC→UCLA CS as the primary test path. The seed includes 94 courses, 51 articulation agreements, 8 prerequisites, and 4 playbooks (3 verified/transferred, 1 in_progress - meeting the requirement of at least 2 verified and 1 unverified). Vitest is configured with React Testing Library and jsdom. All validators pass.
sharedStateObservations: The worker discovered that Supabase CLI can be used to link and push migrations to cloud projects, which is useful for future database operations.
The project uses modern Next.js 15.5.15 with React 19.2.4 - this is at the cutting edge and should be documented as a convention for future workers.
The worker followed the established pattern of placing Supabase client utilities in src/utils/supabase/ with server.ts, client.ts, and middleware.ts files.
The database schema uses UUIDs with gen_random_uuid() for primary keys and includes comprehensive indexes for performance.
Seed data uses deterministic UUIDs for referential integrity across tables - this pattern should be maintained for future seed data.
The Vitest configuration includes @testing-library/jest-dom matchers via vitest.setup.ts - this pattern enables familiar DOM assertions in tests.

commandsRun.observation: 1 test passing: Supabase client browser client creation
Commit e9251a3 contains the full project initialization with 35 files changed
Next.js 15.5.15, TypeScript 5.x, Tailwind CSS v4 configured

interactiveChecks.action: Reviewed project structure and verified all required files exist
interactiveChecks.observed: All required files present: next.config.ts, tsconfig.json (strict mode), vitest.config.ts, vitest.setup.ts, src/utils/supabase/*.ts, src/middleware.ts, src/types/database.ts, supabase/migrations/*.sql

interactiveChecks.action: Verified database schema includes all 9 required tables
interactiveChecks.observed: Tables: institutions, courses, articulation_agreements, prerequisites, profiles, transfer_plans, plan_courses, failure_events, playbooks

8 CC-to-university paths (SMC→UCLA CS, De Anza→SJSU SE, PCC→USC Business, SMC→UCB EECS, LBCC→CSULB CS, Pierce→UCSD CS, De Anza→UCB EECS, PCC→UCSD CS). 13 institutions (7 CCs, 6 universities). 94 courses. 51 articulation agreements. 8 prerequisites. 4 playbooks (3 verified/transferred, 1 in_progress).

server.ts uses createServerClient with cookies for SSR, client.ts uses createBrowserClient for browser, middleware.ts has updateSession with protected route logic (/dashboard, /plan, /playbooks/submit)
vitest.config.ts has jsdom environment, @testing-library/react, globals enabled, passWithNoTests, path alias @/ pointing to ./src

codeReview.summary: The implementation is comprehensive and well-structured. All required components are present and working correctly. The database schema is properly normalized with appropriate foreign keys and indexes. Seed data exceeds requirements with 8 transfer paths including the primary SMC→UCLA CS path. The TypeScript types in src/types/database.ts match the database schema exactly.
